MINI MINUTES FOR APR-16-2021 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RASIER, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUMENT IS HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RASIER, LLC'S DEMURRERS TO FIVE OF THE COMPLAINT'S NUMBERED CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED OR OVERRULED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES OR ORDER SIGNED THIS DATE FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RASIER, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. ARGUMENT IS HELD. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED STRIKING THE SENTENCE REGARDING FRAUD: DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RASIER, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Walt Cubberly, Esq. of Williams Hart Boundas Easterby LLP, 713.230.2200/wcubberly@whlaw.com, for Plaintiff;
Atty. Robert Atkins, Esq. of Paul Weiss Rifkind Wharton Garrison LLP, 212.373.3000, for Defendants Uber and Raiser, LLC;
Atty. Gary Wolenski, Esq. of Buchalter, 949.760.1121/gwolensky@buchalter.com, for Defendants Uber and Raiser, LLC.

DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

Matters called. ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as modified striking the sentence regarding fraud:

DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RASIER, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's motion to strike is denied in part and granted in part. Plaintiff's "allegations for the purpose of prejudicing Uber" are colorful, but within the realm of acceptable pleading, particularly for background. Because the basis for vicarious liability is adequately pled on the basis of the common carrier allegations, that part of the motion is denied. Because the basis for punitive damages is pled with insufficient particularity, that part of the motion is granted with leave to amend.

Counsel for the defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-16-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
This matter is called with case nos. CGC-20-584648 (Line 7) and CGC-20-585158 (Line 8).

Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Walt Cubberly, Esq. of Williams Hart Boundas Easterby LLP, 713.230.2200/wcubberly@whlaw.com, for Plaintiff;
Atty. Robert Atkins, Esq. of Paul Weiss Rifkind Wharton Garrison LLP, 212.373.3000, for Defendants Uber and Raiser, LLC;
Atty. Gary Wolenski, Esq. of Buchalter, 949.760.1121/gwolensky@buchalter.com, for Defendants Uber and Raiser, LLC.

DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RASIER, LLC DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matters called. ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANTS UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC. AND RASIER, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's demurrers to five of the complaint's numbered causes of action are sustained or overruled as follows:

4-7 (assault, sexual battery, false imprisonment, intentional infliction of emotional distress). Defendants' demurrer ignores the complaint's allegations that "Uber is a common carrier" and thus liable for assaults "regardless of whether such acts were committed within the course and scope of employment for Uber." (Cmpl., 17:17-25.) Defendants argue that "Uber cannot be held vicariously liable for Doe One's acts, even if he were an employee or agent (which he is not), since he acted on personal motivations unrelated to his duties as an ultrasound technician." (Dem., 8:23-25.) These factual issues are not for resolution at the pleading stage. The complaint's allegations are sufficiently pled for factual development. This demurrer is overruled.

9 (Ralph Act). The first amended complaint does not adequately plead the elements of a Ralph Act claim as to the demurring defendants, and Plaintiff fails to show on opposition that she can amend to do so. (Cmpl., 30:10-31:7; CACI 3063.) This demurrer is sustained without leave to amend.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)
